summary: Gruber analyzes Apple's blockbuster executive shakeup in which Scott Forstall was pushed out and Jony Ive was given oversight of all human interface design across hardware and software. He traces the internal tensions over skeuomorphic design, noting Forstall's advocacy for it versus Ive's apparent distaste. Gruber acknowledges Forstall's enormous contributions to iOS while arguing the lack of any kind words from Tim Cook in the press release confirms this was not voluntary. He reads the corporate-speak headline about 'increasing collaboration' as plainspoken truth: Forstall was an obstacle to collaboration. The piece frames Ive's expanded role as the most significant outcome, settling the question of who owns design taste at post-Jobs Apple.
key_points: Forstall was pushed out, not departing voluntarily — the absence of any grateful quote from Tim Cook in the press release is the tell
Jony Ive now oversees all product design, hardware and software, which Gruber considers the biggest organizational change at Apple since Jobs's death
Forstall championed skeuomorphic design with Jobs's backing, creating internal tension with Ive and others who opposed the direction
Despite the ouster, Forstall deserves credit for leading iOS from inception and advocating for OS X as the iPhone's foundation
The firing of retail chief John Browett in the same announcement shows Cook's willingness to recognize and correct hiring mistakes
money_quotes: Forstall is not walking away; he was pushed.
No one is more excited than I am to see Jony Ive's design taste spread to Apple's software, but under Forstall's leadership, iOS has been an unprecedented success.
I don't think it can be overstated just how big a deal it is that he now oversees all product design, hardware and software.
That question has now been answered: Jony Ive.
I also wonder how much Forstall was effectively protected by his close relationship with Steve Jobs — protection which, obviously, no longer exists.
Forstall was an obstacle to collaboration within the company.
I wouldn't read anything more into Browett's departure than that Tim Cook knows how to recognize a mistake and correct it.
key_insight: Forstall's ouster and Ive's elevation to oversee all design — hardware and software — is the clearest signal yet of where the buck stops for product taste at post-Jobs Apple.
